gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The externally-tagged wire projection of [`Payload`] — `{\"bits\": \"…\"}`, `{\"trits\": \"…\"}`,"] # [doc = " `{\"scalars\": […]}`, `{\"hypervector\": […]}` — the paradigm-specific `payload` of"] # [doc = " `value.schema.json`."] # [derive (Serialize , Deserialize)] enum PayloadWire { # [serde (rename = "bits")] Bits (String) , # [serde (rename = "trits")] Trits (String) , # [serde (rename = "scalars")] Scalars (Vec < f64 >) , # [serde (rename = "hypervector")] Hypervector (Vec < f64 >) , # [doc = " A scalar-float payload renders as a **string** (`\"1.5\"`, `\"-0.0\"`, `\"inf\"`, `\"-inf\"`,"] # [doc = " `\"NaN\"`), not a JSON number: JSON numbers cannot carry the in-band IEEE specials"] # [doc = " (ADR-040 §2.4), and `serde_json` would serialize a non-finite `f64` as `null` — a silent"] # [doc = " loss (G2). Finite values use Rust's shortest round-trip decimal (`{:?}` — exact by the"] # [doc = " std round-trip guarantee, `Declared`; re-checked by a property test); a malformed string"] # [doc = " is rejected on the way in, never coerced. ADR-040 (M-896)."] # [serde (rename = "float")] Float (String) , # [doc = " A sequence payload renders as a JSON array of self-describing element [`Value`]s — each"] # [doc = " element round-trips through its own `Value` (de)serialization, so the seq is checked"] # [doc = " element-wise on the way in. RFC-0032 D3 (M-749)."] # [serde (rename = "seq")] Seq (Vec < Value >) , # [doc = " A byte payload renders as a lowercase hex string (`\"deadbeef\"`), compact and exactly"] # [doc = " round-trippable; a non-hex / odd-length string is rejected on the way in (never-silent)."] # [doc = " RFC-0032 D4 (M-750)."] # [serde (rename = "bytes")] Bytes (String) , }

("{x:?}")) , Payload :: Seq (elems) => PayloadWire :: Seq (elems . clone ()) , Payload :: Bytes (bytes) => { let mut s = String :: with_capacity (bytes . len () * 2) ; for & b in bytes { use core :: fmt :: Write as _ ; let _ = write ! (s , "{b:02x}") ; } PayloadWire :: Bytes (s) } } ; wire . serialize (serializer) } }

("bit string has non-0/1 char {other:?}"))) , }) . collect :: < Result < Vec < bool > , _ > > () ? ; Payload :: Bits (bits) } PayloadWire :: Trits (s) => { let trits = s . chars () . map (| c | { Trit :: from_wire_char (c) . ok_or_else (| | { Error :: custom (format ! ("trit string has non-+0- char {c:?}")) }) }) . collect :: < Result < Vec < Trit > , _ > > () ? ; Payload :: Trits (trits) } PayloadWire :: Scalars (xs) => Payload :: Scalars (xs) , PayloadWire :: Hypervector (xs) => Payload :: Hypervector (xs) , PayloadWire :: Float (s) => { let x = s . parse :: < f64 > () . map_err (| e | { Error :: custom (format ! ("float string {s:?} is not a valid f64: {e}")) }) ? ; Payload :: Float (x) } PayloadWire :: Seq (elems) => Payload :: Seq (elems) , PayloadWire :: Bytes (s) => { if s . len () % 2 != 0 { return Err (Error :: custom (format ! ("byte string hex has odd length {} (expected an even number of hex digits)" , s . len ()))) ; } let mut bytes = Vec :: with_capacity (s . len () / 2) ; let raw = s . as_bytes () ; let hex_val = | c : u8 | -> Result < u8 , D :: Error > { match c { b'0' ..= b'9' => Ok (c - b'0') , b'a' ..= b'f' => Ok (c - b'a' + 10) , other => Err (Error :: custom (format ! ("byte string has non-hex char {:?}" , other as char))) , } } ; for pair in raw . chunks_exact (2) { bytes . push ((hex_val (pair [0]) ? << 4) | hex_val (pair [1]) ?) ; } Payload :: Bytes (bytes) } }) } }

gaps.snippet: impl Value { # [doc = " Build a value, checking [`Repr::check_well_formed`] (positivity, non-empty model, and the"] # [doc = " [`crate::repr::MAX_DIM`] over-allocation cap) and that `payload` matches `repr`. (`meta` is"] # [doc = " already invariant-checked by [`Meta::new`].)"] pub fn new (repr : Repr , payload : Payload , meta : Meta) -> Result < Self , WfError > { repr . check_well_formed () ? ; if ! payload_matches (& repr , & payload) { return Err (WfError :: PayloadReprMismatch) ; } let payload = match payload { Payload :: Float (x) => Payload :: Float (canonical_float (x)) , other => other , } ; Ok (Value { repr , payload , meta , }) } # [doc = " The representation descriptor."] # [must_use] pub fn repr (& self) -> & Repr { & self . repr } # [doc = " The payload."] # [must_use] pub fn payload (& self) -> & Payload { & self . payload } # [doc = " The metadata."] # [must_use] pub fn meta (& self) -> & Meta { & self . meta } # [doc = " The scalar of a [`Repr::Float`] value, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — a non-float has no scalar here, G2; ADR-040 / M-896). `Exact`: a total"] # [doc = " decidable query. The returned NaN, if any, carries the canonical bits"] # [doc = " ([`CANONICAL_NAN_BITS`]) by the construction invariant of [`Value::new`]."] # [must_use] pub fn float (& self) -> Option < f64 > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Float (x) => Some (* x) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The element count of a [`Repr::Seq`] value, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — a non-sequence has no length here, G2). `Exact`: a total decidable query."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_len (& self) -> Option < usize > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => Some (elems . len ()) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent indexed access into a [`Repr::Seq`] value (RFC-0032 D3): the `i`-th element, or"] # [doc = " `None` when `i` is out of bounds **or** the value is not a sequence — **never** a panic or a"] # [doc = " silent default (G2). `Exact`: total over its domain. The `.myc` `Vec::get` surface bottoms"] # [doc = " out on this."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_get (& self , i : usize) -> Option < & Value > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => elems . get (i) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The elements of a [`Repr::Seq`] value as a slice, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (the fold/iterate basis — RFC-0032 D3). Never-silent: a non-sequence yields `None`, not an"] # [doc = " empty slice."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_elems (& self) -> Option < & [Value] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => Some (elems) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The byte length of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — a non-bytes value has no byte length here). `Exact`. RFC-0032 D4."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_len (& self) -> Option < usize > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => Some (b . len ()) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent indexed byte access into a [`Repr::Bytes`] value (RFC-0032 D4): the `i`-th byte,"] # [doc = " or `None` when `i` is out of bounds **or** the value is not a byte string — **never** a panic"] # [doc = " or a silent default (G2). `Exact`: total over its domain."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_get (& self , i : usize) -> Option < u8 > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => b . get (i) . copied () , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent byte sub-slice `[start, end)` of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value (RFC-0032 D4): `None`"] # [doc = " when the range is out of bounds or inverted, or the value is not a byte string — **never** a"] # [doc = " panic or a silently-clamped range (G2). `Exact`."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_slice (& self , start : usize , end : usize) -> Option < & [u8] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) if start <= end && end <= b . len () => Some (& b [start .. end]) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The bytes of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value as a slice, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — not an empty slice). RFC-0032 D4."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes (& self) -> Option < & [u8] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => Some (b) , _ => None , } } }

gaps.snippet: fn payload_matches (repr : & Repr , payload : & Payload) -> bool { match (repr , payload) { (Repr :: Binary { width } , Payload :: Bits (b)) => b . len () == * width as usize , (Repr :: Ternary { trits } , Payload :: Trits (t)) => t . len () == * trits as usize , (Repr :: Dense { dim , .. } , Payload :: Scalars (s)) => s . len () == * dim as usize , (Repr :: Vsa { dim , .. } , Payload :: Hypervector (h)) => h . len () == * dim as usize , (Repr :: Float { .. } , Payload :: Float (_)) => true , (Repr :: Seq { elem , len } , Payload :: Seq (elems)) => { elems . len () == * len as usize && elems . iter () . all (| e | e . repr () == elem . as_ref ()) } (Repr :: Bytes , Payload :: Bytes (_)) => true , _ => false , } }
